summary: End-to-end testing of admin user & organisation management. Backend: 17 pytest tests passed, 1 skipped (intentional — admin self-delete protection missing). Frontend: admin login → /admin → Gebruikers tab (22 rows, search filters correctly to 1 row, edit modal opens, fields present) → Organisaties tab (18 rows, search filters correctly to 1 row, delete works with cascade). All required data-testids present. Auth: non-admin (lotte) and anonymous correctly receive 401/403 on all /admin/* endpoints. Cascade verified: deleting a test org also deleted its sole user.

critical.issue: Admin can delete themselves — no self-protection check. The seed_admin will be re-created on next backend startup, but during the same session the admin would lose access. Recommend adding: if user_id == admin['id']: raise HTTPException(400, 'Je kan jezelf niet verwijderen').

minor.issue: AdminOrgUpdate.status Literal does not include 'rejected' (only pending/validated/active/inactive). Backend models.py line 289 — inconsistent with OrgStatus enum line 28 which includes 'rejected'. Admin cannot revert an org to 'rejected' via PATCH.
Returns full user docs without password but still leaks 'emailPreferences' and 'updatedAt' fields; minor — consider explicit projection.

Does not delete the user's pending applications nor the listings they applied to. Their applications remain in DB orphaned (no cascade in admin_delete_user). Their listings ARE archived (correct), but applications cleanup missing.

issues: Uses native window.confirm() for delete confirmation instead of styled modal — inconsistent with rest of app's Nederlandse UI/UX style. Consider a custom confirm dialog with localized button labels.
Delete button styled as plain red text-link rather than a button — may be missed; consider btn-destructive style.

action_items: Add self-delete protection in DELETE /api/admin/users/{user_id} — return 400 when user_id == admin['id'].
Extend AdminOrgUpdate.status Literal to include 'rejected' for parity with OrgStatus.
On admin_delete_user: also delete db.applications where applicantUserId == user_id (currently orphaned).
Consider replacing window.confirm() in AdminPanel.jsx with a styled confirmation modal.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py admin_delete_user (line 1308): no protection against admin self-delete; admin@inlimbo.be can DELETE themselves and become locked out until backend restart re-seeds the admin.
server.py admin_delete_user does not cascade-delete applications. db.applications.delete_many({'applicantUserId': user_id}) should be added next to the listings update.
server.py admin_delete_organisation correctly cascades: archives listings + deletes users + deletes org. Verified end-to-end via test_admin_delete_org_cascades and via UI.
models.py AdminOrgUpdate.status Literal (line 289) is missing 'rejected'; mismatched with OrgStatus enum (line 28).
server.py admin_update_user/admin_update_organisation use proper exclude_unset semantics via per-field 'is not None' checks — correct.
AdminPanel.jsx uses native window.confirm — not ideal UX but functional.
Backend correctly emits 401 (not authenticated) for anonymous and 403 (not admin) for non-admin on /admin/* endpoints.

success_rate.backend: 100% (17/17 active, 1 intentionally skipped to flag self-delete)

test_credentials: admin@inlimbo.be / Admin123! (admin). lotte@atelier-brussel.example / User123! (non-admin). Test users/orgs created via POST /api/auth/register/new-org with TEST_ prefix and cleaned up after each delete test.
seed_data_creation: Each pytest run creates 2 fresh TEST_du_* and TEST_dorg_* users+orgs (one for user-delete, one for org-delete-cascade test) and cleans them up in the same test. UI session deleted the leftover TEST_org_1779837381 (plus its testpending user via cascade). Remaining test orgs (TEST Org 754e40, TEST Org 88d3f6, TEST_org_e30f99, TEST_org_9723ec) untouched.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file at /app/backend/tests/test_admin_user_org_management.py is regression-ready (17 tests, all passing). Frontend uses div-based row layout with data-testid='admin-user-row-{id}' and 'admin-org-row-{id}' (NOT <table>). Search inputs ('admin-gebruikers-search' / 'admin-organisaties-search') trigger server-side filtering via ?q= when query length >=2, with 300ms debounce. Edit modals contain plain <input type='text'> for firstName/lastName/email/phone and native <select> for role/status. Delete uses window.confirm() — page.on('dialog', lambda d: d.accept()) needed in Playwright. Main agent: please fix the three action items (self-delete protection, AdminOrgUpdate status enum, application cascade).
rca of the issue: 1) Admin self-delete: DELETE /api/admin/users/{user_id} only checks if user exists (line 1311-1313), no equality check against admin['id']. Reproduction: login as admin, curl -X DELETE /api/admin/users/<admin-id> — returns 200, admin doc removed from DB. Mitigation: add `if user_id == admin['id']: raise HTTPException(400, 'Je kan jezelf niet verwijderen')` before the delete_one. 2) Orphaned applications: admin_delete_user archives listings owned by user but leaves applications (where user is applicant) intact. Mitigation: add db.applications.delete_many({'applicantUserId': user_id}) inside admin_delete_user. 3) AdminOrgUpdate status: models.py Literal is restrictive — to revert an org to rejected/pending via admin edit, the literal must include those states.
